title: Relevance Judgments and Retrieval Metrics · Runtime Lens: Rag Retrieval Comparison Uncertainty
summary: A dashboard reports average similarity as retrieval accuracy, has one relevant document per query, ignores unjudged results, and compares systems at different cutoffs. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the retrieval comparison uncertainty increment in retrieval evaluation notebook and judgment policy. Learners…
objectives: Use paired per-query differences, confidence intervals or resampling, practical thresholds, and regression inspection when comparing retrievers.
Report metrics by language, intent, difficulty, freshness, tenant, source type, answerability, and harm instead of only one mean.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: rag-retrieval-comparison-uncertainty — Use paired per-query differences, confidence intervals or resampling, practical thresholds, and regression inspection when comparing retrievers.
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-retrieval-metrics-2
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-retrieval-metrics-2.
# Case 7417: a regulated document deletion request.
# Operating constraint: record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall.
def worked_rag_retrieval_metrics_2(relevant_retrieved: int = 3, retrieved: int = 5, relevant_total: int = 4, reciprocal_rank: float = 0.5, graded_gain: float = 0.8) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "query2dekrow",
        "tenant7417",
        "indexworked27417",
        "prompt2dekrow7417",
        "evaluation7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if min(relevant_retrieved, retrieved, relevant_total) < 0:
        return False, 0.0, "negative-count"
    if relevant_retrieved > retrieved or relevant_retrieved > relevant_total:
        return False, 0.0, "judgment-count"
    if not 0.0 <= reciprocal_rank <= 1.0 or not 0.0 <= graded_gain <= 1.0:
        return False, 0.0, "metric-range"
    precision = relevant_retrieved / retrieved if retrieved else 0.0
    recall = relevant_retrieved / relevant_total if relevant_total else 0.0
    balanced = 2 * precision * recall / (precision + recall) if precision + recall else 0.0
    assert 0.0 <= balanced <= 1.0
    return True, round((balanced + reciprocal_rank + graded_gain) / 3, 4), "metric-vector"

# Evidence: rag-retrieval-comparison-uncertainty-my96n8

    evidence_variant_my96n8 = "my96n8-8n69ym-5894"
    assert evidence_variant_my96n8

        "corpusmy96n8",
        "query8n69ym",

    if min(relevant_retrieved, retrieved, relevant_total) < 0:
        return False, 0.0, "negative-count"
    if relevant_retrieved > retrieved or relevant_retrieved > relevant_total:
        return False, 0.0, "judgment-count"
    if not 0.0 <= reciprocal_rank <= 1.0 or not 0.0 <= graded_gain <= 1.0:

    precision = relevant_retrieved / retrieved if retrieved else 0.0

    balanced = 2 * precision * recall / (precision + recall) if precision + recall else 0.0
    assert 0.0 <= balanced <= 1.0
    return True, round((balanced + reciprocal_rank + graded_gain) / 3, 4), "metric-vector"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Rag Retrieval Comparison Uncertainty structural check is only one gate. retrieval evaluation notebook and judgment policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For RAG and Python Build Rag Retrieval Comparison Uncertainty evidence · Theory Rag Retrieval Compariso…, name the stakeholder, corpus, source, document, chunk, model, index, query, relevance, tenant, context, citation, evaluation, and transfer boundary, Rag Retrieval Comparison Uncertainty precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RAG and Python Build Rag Retrieval Comparison Uncertainty evidence · Theory Rag Retrieval Compariso…, trace this concrete input and predict ingestion, normalization, retrieval, filter, fusion, reranking, context, claim, citation, abstention, latency, cost, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory RAG engineering team handles case 3658 during a public-benefits policy change. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate precision, recall, reciprocal rank, graded gain, cutoff, and judgment assumptions; retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity; then insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use paired per-query differences, confidence intervals or resampling, practical thresholds, and regression inspection when comparing retrievers.

For RAG and Python Build Rag Retrieval Comparison Uncertainty evidence · Theory Rag Retrieval Compariso…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-query, changed-corpus, relevance, retrieval, grounding, citation, adversarial, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native or production-transfer evidence: A one-point metric increase on one fixed set is automatically a meaningful win.

steps.title: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Retrieval Comparison Uncertainty model · Theory Rag Retrieval Compari…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Retrieval Comparison Uncertainty: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 5277 during an enterprise retrieval migration canary. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate precision, recall, reciprocal rank, graded gain, cutoff, and judgment assumptions; record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use paired per-query differences, confidence intervals or resampling, practical thresholds, and regression inspection when comparing retrievers.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Retrieval Comparison Uncertainty model · Theory Rag Retrieval Compari….
steps.why: Retrieving Rag Retrieval Comparison Uncertainty through the concrete retrieval evaluation notebook and judgment policy case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Retrieval Comparison Uncertainty model · Theory Rag Retrieval Compari…, keep Rag Retrieval Comparison Uncertainty connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use paired per-query differences, confidence intervals or resampling, practical thresholds, and regression inspection when comparing retrievers.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: rag-retrieval-comparison-uncertainty-my96n8(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_rag_retrieval_comparison_uncertainty_my96n8\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?relevant_retrieved > retrieved)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?reciprocal_rank <= 1.0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?precision =)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?recall =)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
